meaning: n.撅嘴,板脸,生气,大头鱼类v.撅嘴,绷脸
examples: For more of a pout, imagine your boyfriend has been away for a month.
Her lips were set in a pout of annoyance.
Smith had listened to the question intently with his lips pressed into a thin pout.
For lips, shelve the ChapStick and grab Kaplan MD's LIP 20 Mask, which repairs that delicate skin and plumps your pout.
Being the professional he is, Murray does as many stunts as he can, leaving the stunt man to pout in the corner.
